title: Roasted Chewy Mexican Pork Tacos Al Pastor
description: A traditional Sonoran dish featuring marinated pork, roasted to perfection and served in tacos al pastor. This recipe is dairy-free friendly and can be made in under an hour.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine lime juice, orange juice, ginger, cumin, coriander, paprika, garlic, ancho chili powder, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Place the pork shoulder in a large zip-top plastic bag. Pour the marinade over the pork and seal the bag. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the pork from the marinade and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Place the pork on a rimmed baking sheet or a slate tile. Roast in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, reduce the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C). Continue roasting for an additional 2 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Transfer the pork to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Shred the pork with two forks and transfer it to a bowl. Strain the juices from the pan and discard the solids. Add the strained juices to the bowl with the pork and toss to combine.
Warm tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by placing a portion of the pork onto a tortilla and topping with diced pineapple, red onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice.
